primitive-types: Rust primitive types (integers, floats, booleans, char), numeric literals, and type inference rules
variables-immutability: Rust bindings with let, let mut, const, mutability restrictions, and variable shadowing
data-structures: Rust data structures: arrays, vectors, tuples, and hash maps with appropriate use cases
module-system: Rust module system with paths, use statements, pub visibility, and crate organization
conditionals: Rust if, else if, else, and using if as an expression that returns a value
loops: Rust loop, while, and for loops with break, continue, ranges, and iteration patterns
match: Rust match expressions, pattern branches, wildcards, guards, and exhaustive control flow
functions: Rust function declarations, parameters, return expressions, methods, associated functions, and the self receiver
structs: Rust struct definitions, struct instances, tuple structs, unit structs, and field update patterns
enums: Rust enum definitions, variants with data, match expressions, and if let on enum values
traits: Rust trait definitions, impl blocks, trait bounds, default methods, and shared behavior across types
ownership: Rust ownership rules, stack vs heap memory, moves, Copy types, and ownership transfer
borrowing: Rust borrowing with immutable and mutable references, aliasing rules, and the borrow checker
slices: Rust slices, string slices, slice syntax, and borrowing contiguous data without taking ownership
string-vs-str: Rust String vs &str, owned versus borrowed text, string literals, conversion patterns, and API design choices for text
lifetimes: Rust lifetime annotations, borrow checker rules, reference validity, and returned borrows
results: Rust Result<T, E> values, Ok/Err variants, error propagation, and explicit recoverable failure handling
options: Rust Option<T> values, Some/None variants, and explicit handling of optional data
error-handling: Rust error handling with the question mark operator, match, if let, panic vs recoverable errors, and targeted use of unwrap
custom-errors: Rust custom error enums, the std::error::Error trait, Display implementations, From conversions, and when thiserror or anyhow is appropriate
generics: Rust generic types, functions, methods, trait-bound constraints, and compile-time monomorphization
closures: Rust closures, environment capture, move closures, and the distinctions between Fn, FnMut, and FnOnce
iterators: Rust iterators with iter, iter_mut, into_iter, lazy adapters like map and filter, and collect-based pipelines
lifetimes-advanced: Rust lifetime elision rules, generic lifetimes, multiple borrows, and lifetime relationships between references
smart-pointers: Rust smart pointers: Box, Rc, Arc, Cell, and RefCell, what capability each adds, and when to choose each one
concurrency: Rust concurrency with threads, channels, Arc<Mutex<T>>, and the roles of Send and Sync
macros: Rust macros with macro_rules!, derive macros, and when macros are preferable to functions or generics
